question: What's the best time to visit Port Stephens NSW with kids?
answer: G'day! Port Stephens is brilliant year-round, but April-May and September-October are absolute crackers for families. You'll dodge the school holiday crowds and prices, plus the weather's still gorgeous for beach days. Summer (Dec-Feb) is peak season – perfect for swimming but book early! Winter's quieter and great for dolphin watching, though pack a jumper for those cooler evenings.

question: Are there pet-friendly holiday parks in Port Stephens NSW?
answer: Too right there are! Several holiday parks around Port Stephens welcome your furry mates. NRMA Port Stephens Salamander Bay and Reflections Holiday Parks Jimmys Beach both offer pet-friendly cabins and sites. Just check their specific pet policies beforehand – most require dogs on leads and have designated pet areas. Your four-legged family member will love the beaches too, especially off-leash Samurai Beach!

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Port Stephens NSW during school holidays?
answer: School holiday rates definitely jump up, mate. Expect powered sites from $60-90 per night, while family cabins range from $180-350+ per night depending on size and facilities. Easter and Christmas holidays are priciest. Pro tip: book 6-12 months ahead for better rates and availability. Mid-week stays are often cheaper than weekends, even during school hols!

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Besides the usual clothes and toiletries, don't forget: sunscreen and hats (absolute must!), swimmers and towels, insect repellent, kids' bikes or scooters, beach toys, a first aid kit, and footy or frisbee. Most cabins have kitchens, so BYO basics. Pack games for rainy days, nightlights for little ones, and a portable phone charger. Oh, and esky for day trips!

question: What is there to do near Port Stephens NSW holiday parks for families?
answer: Heaps of ripper activities! Hit the gorgeous beaches – Shoal Bay and Fingal Bay are family favourites. Go dolphin watching (99% sighting success!), try sandboarding at Stockton Dunes, or visit Oakvale Farm for animal encounters. Tomaree Head Summit walk offers stunning views, and Nelson Bay has great fish and chips. Many parks have pools, playgrounds and jumping pillows too!
